title: Fix footer font colours and hamburger/filter menu icon colours for readability

requirements.summary: Update all text colours in the footer section to high-contrast, readable colours suitable for the dark background
acceptanceCriteria: All text in the footer is clearly readable against the footer background
Section headings, body text, links, and labels in the footer all use a suitable high-contrast colour
No footer text appears invisible or low-contrast against its background

file_operations.description: Update all text colour classes in the Footer component to ensure high contrast against the dark background. Change brand info text, section headings (Quick Links, Our Menu, Contact Us), navigation links, menu category links, contact details, and newsletter text to use light/white colours (e.g., text-white, text-gray-100, text-gray-200) for maximum readability. Ensure hover states maintain good contrast.

requirements.summary: Update the hamburger menu icon colour in the mobile navbar to high-contrast for clear visibility
acceptanceCriteria: The three-line (hamburger) icon in the mobile navbar is clearly visible against the navbar background
The icon colour is consistent with the overall design palette and readable at all scroll states

file_operations.description: Update the colour of the hamburger menu icon (three-line button) in the mobile navigation section to ensure high contrast against the navbar background. Apply suitable colour classes (e.g., text-white, text-gray-100, or theme-appropriate contrast colours) to the icon SVG or button element. Ensure the colour works well in both scrolled and non-scrolled navbar states.

requirements.summary: Update filter tab colours in MenuTabs to readable, high-contrast colours for both active and inactive states
acceptanceCriteria: All filter tab labels in MenuTabs are clearly readable in both active and inactive states
Tab text and icons have sufficient contrast against the tab bar background
Active tab is visually distinguishable from inactive tabs with readable colours

file_operations.description: Update the text and background colour classes for both active and inactive tab states in the MenuTabs component. Ensure inactive tabs use a high-contrast text colour (e.g., text-gray-700, text-gray-800) against their background, and active tabs use a contrasting colour scheme (e.g., dark text on light background or light text on dark background) that clearly distinguishes them while maintaining readability. Adjust any icon colours to match the text contrast requirements.
